terms.definition: P(A): the probability of hypothesis A before observing evidence B; the starting belief in a Bayes update.
P(A|B): the updated probability of A after observing evidence B, computed via Bayes' theorem.
Replacing a prior with a posterior using P(θ|x) ∝ P(x|θ)P(θ) after new data x are observed.
A prior whose functional form is unchanged after multiplying by the likelihood; the posterior belongs to the same family (e.g., Beta prior with Bernoulli likelihood).
P(x) = ∫ P(x|θ)P(θ) dθ; normalizes the posterior and measures overall support for the data under the model.
The mode of the posterior argmax_θ P(θ|x); reduces to MLE when the prior is flat over the relevant range.
A prior intended to impart minimal preference among θ values, letting the likelihood dominate—though true non-informativeness depends on parameterization.
